title: Absent Teacher
situationText: When you arrive at school, you find out that your teacher is sick and a substitute teacher will be taking the class today.

ideal.text: I ask what the class will be like and try to get to know the new teacher.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You have a better understanding of what to expect.
ideal.laterConsequence: The day goes by more smoothly.

intermediary.text: I feel uncomfortable, but I participate in the activities normally.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: It takes you a little while to adapt.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You manage to keep up with most of the class.

impulsive.text: I spend the class complaining because I don't like the change.
impulsive.immediateConsequence: You become more irritated during the morning.
impulsive.laterConsequence: You enjoy the proposed activities less.

title: Canceled Recess
situationText: It is raining heavily and outdoor recess has been canceled.

ideal.text: I look for another fun activity to do inside the school.
ideal.immediateConsequence: You find something interesting to do.

intermediary.text: I feel sad, but I wait for recess to end.
intermediary.immediateConsequence: Time seems to pass more slowly.
intermediary.laterConsequence: You manage to get through the rest of the day.

impulsive.text: I say it's unfair and refuse to participate in any activities.
impulsive.laterConsequence: The rest of the period becomes more difficult.
